title: Starmer’s downfall: Is the media to blame?
gist: A short podcast episode blurb (Media Confidential) flagging discussion of Keir Starmer's resignation and the politician-media relationship, the UK government's green paper on public service media (BBC, ITV, YouTube, Facebook), and illness in public life.

insights.claim: The episode frames the modern politician-media relationship as adversarial survival — how a politician feeds the media without being eaten — in the context of Starmer's resignation.
insights.speaker: Prospect Magazine
insights.so_what: Signals media-handling is viewed as a determinant of UK political survival, relevant to assessing Burnham's prospects.
insights.quote: How does a modern politician feed the media—and avoid being eaten?

insights.claim: The UK government has issued a green paper on public service media addressing the roles of the BBC, ITV, YouTube and Facebook.
insights.speaker: Prospect Magazine
insights.so_what: A live UK media-regulation policy process worth tracking for impact on broadcasters and platform obligations.
insights.quote: the government’s green paper on public service media and the role of BBC, ITV, YouTube, and Facebook
